description: The venv was broken (created with path /Users/frakbox/trading_bot2/venv which doesn't exist). Had to recreate venv and install all packages manually. Took ~15 minutes of debugging.
potential_fix: In CLAUDE.md or smoke_test.py, add a venv path check and auto-recreate if broken. Or include a requirements.txt with all specific pinned versions that work together (alpaca-trade-api has strict conflicts with urllib3/websockets).

description: alpaca-trade-api 3.2.0 has very strict dependency requirements (msgpack==1.0.3, PyYAML==6.0.1, websockets<11) that conflict with yfinance requirements. Had to install with --no-deps and then add missing packages one by one.
potential_fix: Pin all dependency versions in requirements.txt. Consider replacing alpaca-trade-api with alpaca-py (the newer SDK that doesn't have these conflicts). File: requirements.txt

description: measure_event_impact() returns flat keys like avg_abnormal_1d, not nested dict like result['1d']. Had to inspect the return structure before using it. CLAUDE.md API quick reference is misleading - shows nested dict syntax that doesn't match actual API.
potential_fix: Update CLAUDE.md API Quick Reference to show actual flat key structure: result['avg_abnormal_1d'] not result['1d']['mean_abnormal_return_pct']. Or add a convenience wrapper.

description: measure_event_impact individual_impacts don't include the date field - shows '?' for date. Makes it impossible to cross-reference events with their dates without re-running.
potential_fix: Fix market_data.py to include the event date in individual_impacts dict. Should be trivial to add 'date' key to the per-event result.

description: yfinance returns empty data for delisted tickers (SESN, ATNX, ICPT, CORV). These are the most impactful FDA CRL events. No fallback data source available in the codebase. Caused 33% data quality failure in FDA backtest.
potential_fix: Add Polygon.io or Tiingo API as fallback data source for delisted tickers. Both have historical data for stocks that were delisted/acquired. Add to market_data.py as a secondary data source when yfinance returns no data.

description: Wikipedia S&P 500 component changes table returned 403 Forbidden. Needed to manually search for deletion dates across multiple press releases. Wikipedia would have been a single comprehensive source.
potential_fix: Use S&P Global press release archive (press.spglobal.com) as primary source - it's structured and fetchable. Could build a scraper that finds all S&P 500 rebalance announcements by pattern-matching press release URLs.
